extra.message: User-controlled params['id'] is concatenated directly into SQL query passed to cursor.execute() without parameterization. Allows extracting any data from the database via UNION, boolean-based blind, or time-based blind SQL injection.

User-controlled params['comment'] is interpolated into SQL INSERT via string formatting ('%s'). Allows SQL injection to manipulate database contents, extract data via subqueries, or trigger second-order attacks.

The password parameter is concatenated into SQL query without any sanitization (username is partially sanitized via regex but password has no filtering). Allows authentication bypass via classic SQL injection (e.g., ' OR '1'='1).
User-controlled params['v'] is inserted into HTML output via regex substitution without any HTML escaping. Allows reflected cross-site scripting (XSS) by injecting arbitrary HTML/JavaScript.

Comment content retrieved from the database is rendered directly into HTML <td> tags without any HTML escaping. Enables stored cross-site scripting (XSS) — any malicious payload stored via the comment endpoint executes when viewing comments.

HTML_PREFIX contains an inline script that reads document.location.hash and writes it directly to the DOM via document.write() without any sanitization. Allows DOM-based XSS via the URL fragment (hash), e.g., #lang=<img/src=x onerror=alert(1)>.
User-controlled params['domain'] is concatenated into a shell command ('nslookup ' + input) executed via subprocess.run() with shell=True. Allows arbitrary command execution (e.g., domain=; id; #).

User-controlled params['include'] is used to load file contents (locally via open() or remotely via urlopen()) and then passed directly to exec(). Allows arbitrary Python code execution (remote file inclusion / code injection).
User-controlled params['object'] is passed directly to pickle.loads() without any validation. Allows remote code execution via malicious pickled objects.

User-controlled params['path'] is passed to open() with os.path.abspath() which resolves relative paths but does NOT restrict the root directory. Allows reading arbitrary files from the filesystem (e.g., /etc/passwd, application source code).
When params['path'] contains '://', it is passed to urllib.request.urlopen(). Allows server-side request forgery to fetch arbitrary URLs, including internal services and cloud metadata endpoints.
When params['include'] contains '://', it is passed to urllib.request.urlopen(). Allows server-side request forgery to fetch arbitrary remote URLs.

User-controlled params['name'] is interpolated directly into an XPath query via string formatting. Allows XPath injection to bypass authentication logic, extract arbitrary XML data, or cause denial of service.

User-controlled params['xml'] is parsed by lxml with load_dtd=True, resolve_entities=True, and no_network=False. Allows XML External Entity (XXE) attacks to read local files, perform SSRF, or cause denial of service.
User-controlled params['redir'] is inserted directly into a meta http-equiv refresh tag without validation. Allows open redirect to arbitrary external URLs, which can be used for phishing attacks.

On any exception, traceback.format_exc() returns the full Python stack trace to the HTTP response. This leaks sensitive information including file paths, code structure, and potentially database details or variable contents.

cwe: CWE-209: Information Exposure Through an Error Message

extra.message: The /users.json endpoint supports an unvalidated 'callback' parameter that wraps JSON user data in an arbitrary JavaScript function call (JSONP). Enables cross-domain data theft — a malicious site can include this as a script tag and exfiltrate all usernames/surnames.

User-controlled params['charset'] is inserted directly into the Content-Type HTTP header without validation. Allows HTTP header injection / response splitting by injecting CRLF sequences in the charset parameter.

User-controlled params['size'] is used without any upper bound to create a list of 'size' strings each of length 'size' (O(n²) memory). Allows denial of service by exhausting server memory with a large size value.

X-XSS-Protection header is explicitly set to '0', disabling the browser's built-in reflected XSS filter. Combined with actual XSS vulnerabilities in the application, this removes a defense-in-depth control.

Multiple user credentials are hardcoded in the USERS_XML string, including admin password '7en8aiDoh!' and passwords for dricci, amason, and svargas. These credentials are embedded in source code and loaded into the database at startup.

No authentication mechanism is enforced on any endpoint. The /login path sets a SESSIONID cookie on successful authentication (line 68), but there is no middleware, decorator, or explicit check anywhere in do_GET() to validate this session token before serving sensitive data or performing privileged operations. All endpoints (/, /users.json, /login) are accessible without authentication.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function
